original_hypothesis.title: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee Has No Upper Bound Enabling Complete Fee Capture or DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee allows GAUGE_ADMIN or owner to set dibsPercentage to any uint256 value with no upper bound check. Since REFERRAL_FEE_DENOMINATOR is 1000, setting dibsPercentage to 1000 routes 100% of all CL gauge fees to the dibs address, leaving nothing for voters. Setting it above 1000 causes arithmetic underflow in GaugeCL._claimFees (claimed0 -= _dibsFeeToken0 where _dibsFeeToken0 > claimed0), reverting all fee distributions across ALL CL gauges permanently.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: A single admin parameter change can permanently break fee distribution for all CL gauges or redirect all fees to a chosen address.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: deployment-flows/scripts/cl/setReferralFeeInGaugeFactory.js sets it to 500 (50%), which is within valid range but high

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setReferralFee(uint256 _dibsFee) external onlyAllowed { dibsPercentage = _dibsFee; } — no require(_dibsFee < 1000)

preconditions: GAUGE_ADMIN or owner calls setReferralFee with value >= 1000
Any CL gauge subsequently tries to distribute fees via _claimFees

initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Immediate and global — affects all CL gauges

operational_questions: What is the current dibsPercentage value on-chain?
Is there any governance constraint on fee parameter changes?
validation_requirements: Test _claimFees with dibsPercentage = 1001 and verify underflow revert
Check current deployment value matches deployment script (500)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All subsequent _claimFees calls on all CL gauges revert due to underflow
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Fee distribution for ALL CL gauges is permanently blocked
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Fees accumulate in gauges with no way to distribute them
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires GAUGE_ADMIN key compromise or malicious admin

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: All CL gauge fee distribution blocked with single parameter change
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: No voter earns fees from any CL pool
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Admin must set dibsPercentage back to valid value, but accumulated fees during outage may have issues
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Fee distribution for all CL gauges

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setReferralFee calls and current dibsPercentage value
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Failed _claimFees across multiple CL gauges simultaneously
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Parameter changes near fee distribution events

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
